response.justification: Bogdan has impressive breadth as an individual contributor across nearly two decades of software development, with genuine developer-tooling and CI/CD experience at Altom and MCP/agentic infrastructure work at Acme. However, the role is fundamentally a people-leadership position, and there is no evidence anywhere in the resume of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, owning headcount, or leading an engineering team. His 'Staff' titles reflect technical seniority, not organizational management, and 'Founder' of a solo open-source project does not satisfy the 2-year people-management minimum. The technical foundations are present, but the management dimension—the primary qualification—is entirely absent.
strengths: Long, continuous software career (~20 years) with genuine full-stack depth and polyglot fluency across many production environments

Hands-on developer-tooling experience: CI/CD pipelines, mobile device orchestration, test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and MCP code generators align directly with the productivity focus of the role
Knowledge-sharing culture evidenced by public workshops, conference talks, and organizing Code Kata/Refactoring Dojo sessions—traits that map well to engineering development responsibilities
concerns: Zero documented people-management experience: no direct reports, no 1:1s, no performance ownership, no hiring—the role's core requirement is entirely unmet
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, cross-team organizational alignment, or setting team-level priorities at any employer
Career trajectory is consistently as an individual contributor; transition to a mid-to-senior management role would be a large, unvalidated leap with no supporting signal in the resume

key_factors.factor: No evidence of organizational or strategic leadership
